titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert Giard Papers
titlestmt.author: Andrea Benefiel and Molly Wheeler. Revised by Alison Fulmer.

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7151233.
This collection contains 876 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 949 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 01:38

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The papers consist of photographic prints, negatives, contacts sheets, slides, correspondence, diaries, financial papers, and printed materials documenting Robert Giard's career as a photographer and his personal life, spanning the years 1962-2019. The bulk of the papers contain photographic prints by Giard in several sizes of various subjects, including portraits of gay and lesbian writers and activists, landscapes, nudes, still lifes, and portraits of the Thanks Be To Grandmother Winifred Foundation grant recipients.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jonathan G. Silin on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 2004, 2018. Gift of Jonathan G. Silin, 2011, 2019.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Robert Giard Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert Giard Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog for "From the Robert Giard Papers".

bioghist.head: Robert Giard (1939-2002)
bioghist.p: Robert Giard was an American photographer, born in Hartford, Connecticut in 1939. He received a Bachelor of Arts from Yale University in 1961, and Master of Arts in Comparative Literature from Boston University in 1965. In 1972 he began to take photographs, focusing on landscapes and portraits of his friends, artists, writers, and nudes. His monograph Particular Voices: Portraits of Gay and Lesbian Writers, published by MIT Press in 1997, contains a selection from over 500 portraits he had taken in the previous decade. In 1996, Giard was commissioned to document the 321 grant recipients of the Thanks Be To Grandmother Winifred Foundation. The grant recipients were women over the age of 54 whose projects benefitted adult women. From 1996 to 2001, Giard traveled across the U. S. to photograph each year's grant recipients. He died in 2002 of a heart attack.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: 2005 and 2011 Acquisitions: The 2005 and 2011 acquisitions received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization, at the time of acquisition. Further organization, rehousing, and description were carried out in 2013.

These acquisitions are comprised of material formerly classed as: Uncat MSS 765 and Uncat MSS 1274. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, according to month and year of acquisition.

Description of the 2005 and 2011 acquisitions is compiled from individual preliminary lists for each acquisition that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. The preliminary lists were migrated to comply with current archival descriptive standards and merged into a single file in 2007-2008. As part of the migration, modifications were made to the formatting of individual lists; however, the content of the lists was neither modified nor verified. Box 271 contains print-outs of original inventories of 16" x 20" photographs. These inventories contain detailed information not found in this finding aid and originate from a data export of a FileMaker Pro Database acquired with the collection.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.

2018 and 2019 Acquisitions: The 2018 and 2019 acquisitions were arranged, rehoused, and described in winter/spring 2023-2024. The negatives, contact sheets, and slides that Giard housed in binders have been removed from binders and foldered. Folder titles for these materials are derived from the original binder labels. Many negatives from the 1960s-1970s are housed in original negative booklets. Most slides were removed from their original deteriorating plastic sleeves and rehoused in archival sleeves.

The 2018 acquisition was accompanied by a digital spreadsheet export of Giard's Filemaker Pro database which cross-references negatives, contact sheets, and prints of his portraits and some landscapes and sites. This spreadsheet includes negatives and contact sheets from the following subseries: Portraits, Series A, Series B, Series C, and Winifred. The spreadsheet includes prints from the following creator-imposed series: Gay and Lesbian Writers, Gay Sites, Nudes, and Portraits. Links to the Excel spreadsheet and to a Word document with instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet are included in the relevant scope and content notes for negatives and contact sheets.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four groupings: I. February 2005 Acquisition, 1972-2002. II. October 2011 Acquisition, 1988-2001. III. November 2018 Acquisition, 1962-2018. IV. September 2019 Acquisition, 2012-2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of photographic prints, negatives, contacts sheets, slides, correspondence, diaries, financial papers, and printed materials documenting Robert Giard's career as a photographer and his personal life, spanning the years 1962-2019. The bulk of the papers contain photographic prints by Giard in several sizes of various subjects, including portraits of gay and lesbian writers and activists, landscapes, nudes, still lifes, and portraits of the Thanks Be To Grandmother Winifred Foundation grant recipients.

corpname.part: Thanks Be to Grandmother Winifred Foundation

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jonathan G. Silin on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 2004.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This group of material is organized into five series: Correspondence, Professional Papers, Photograph Work Prints, Oversize Photographs (16" x 20"), and Photographs (11" x 14").

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This group contains correspondence, professional papers diaries, financial papers, photographs, printed material, journals, and other related papers.

Box 271 contains print-outs of original inventories of 16" x 20" photographs. These inventories contain detailed information not found in this finding aid and originate from a data export of a FileMaker Pro Database acquired with the collection.

did.unittitle: Personal and professional correspondence including articles on gay writers, publishers, and artists; clippings; postcards and mailings

dao.linktitle: [Folder 2] [ALS] [19]95 Dec. 18, [Honey Lee Cottrell] to Robert Giard. 28.3 x 17.3 cm. [recto]

Personal and professional correspondence including articles on gay writers, publishers, and artists; clippings; postcards and mailings
Personal and professional correspondence including articles on gay writers, publishers, and artists; clippings; postcards and mailings
Miscellaneous diaries and notebooks chronicling Giard's early involvement with photographic ideas and interest in literature, interspersed with news clipping, literary quotes, sketches

descriptivenote.p: Miscellaneous diaries and notebooks chronicling Giard's early involvement with photographic ideas and interest in literature, interspersed with news clipping, literary quotes, sketches

did.unittitle: Papers pertaining to the Gay and Lesbian Writers project, extensive travel notes, and descriptions of the meetings and photographic sessions with the 250 grant recipients
Papers pertaining to the Gay and Lesbian Writers project, extensive travel notes, and descriptions of the meetings and photographic sessions with the 250 grant recipients

Complete set of photocopies of Giard's original diaries related to the Gay and Lesbian Writers Series, photocopies describing meetings and photographic sessions with the writers (original diaries are in the permanent collection of the New York Public Library)
Desk files, weekly calendars, grant proposals, resumes, professional correspondence, exhibition announcements and brochures, obituaries, awards, articles, plans for future projects
Desk files, weekly calendars, grant proposals, resumes, professional correspondence, exhibition announcements and brochures, obituaries, awards, articles, plans for future projects

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Gay and Lesbian Writers, Landscapes, Nudes, Portraits, and Miscellaneous.

arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Gay and Lesbian Writers, Landscapes, Portraits, and Nudes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: LSF# A set of the portfolio inserts, including the colophon and introduction by Gene Thornton, former critic for the New York Times, is included in the Yale package, however the group of prints listed below does not represent the entire portfolio as a print of the image "Two Deer" is not available.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note: A set of the portfolio inserts, including the colophon and introduction by Gene Thornton, former critic for the New York Times, is included in the Yale package, however the group of prints listed above does not represent the entire portfolio as a print of the image "Two Deer" is not available.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine subseries: Portraits, India, Landscapes, Nudes, Balloon Series, Neo-Classical Series, Piano Series, Beach Sequence, and Assorted Nudes.

did.unittitle: George Stambolian with Cape profile right
Man sitting cross legged on bed
Woman with knitted hat from back
Portrait of Richard Zellins (Man in His Loft)
Man in Mickey Mouse Tee Shirt
Boy in a Mickey Mouse Shirt
Mother and Baby in a White Suit
Group of Boys at Billiard Table
Man w/White Glove and Knitted Cap
Woman with Her Hair in a Towel
Black Girl with a White Doll
(two children in driveway, one flying kite)
Trees in Front of a House
(sidewalk, fence, tree at left, house at right)
(small, leafless bush in front of larger hedges)
(rearview of male, arms wrapped around back, painting in background)
(torso at left, painted bird at right-Jonathan Silin in bathroom)
(male nude wearing snorkel mask seated in bathroom)
Motorcycle Nude with Hand on Back
Artist and Model (self-portrait in mirror with Jonathan Silin)
(standing male nude wearing glasses, leaning on outstretched hand)
Man with a Pillow over His Head
Richard in the Shower No. 3: Drying Himself
Richard in the Shower No. 8: Washing His Foot
Richard in the Shower No. 7: With Leg on Edge of Tub
Richard in the Shower No. 6: Holding Towel, Leg Raised
(male nude with foot in sink-Jonathan Silin)
(male standing in doorway with painting in background)
(crouching male nude holding onto mirror with one hand)
Richard in the Shower No. 5: Bending Over
Richard in the Shower No. 9: Standing
Nude with Weight Between His Legs
(nude on tiptoes with foot in sink-Jonathan Silin)
Correspondence, writings of others, and printed materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition contains Giard's negatives, contact sheets, slides, and professional papers.

For more information about subject names and image locations for negatives and contact sheets in the subseries Portraits, Series A, Series B, Series C, and Winifred, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). The spreadsheet is an export of Giard's Filemaker Pro database which cross-references negatives, contact sheets, and prints of his portraits and some landscapes and sites. See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This acquisition is arranged in four series: I. Contact Sheets, II. Negatives, III. Slides, IV. Professional Papers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jonathan G. Silin on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 2018.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains contact sheets of Giard's negatives. For more information about subject names and image locations for contact sheets in the subseries Portraits, Series A, Series B, Series C, and Winifred, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). The spreadsheet is an export of Giard's Filemaker Pro database which cross-references negatives, contact sheets, and prints of his portraits and some landscapes and sites. See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).
Some contact sheets are stored with negatives in Series II: Negatives, Miscellaneous Negatives and Color negatives with contact sheets.

Contacts sheets in folders that are labeled sequentially (i.e. 1 of 2, 2 of 2) were taken from the same binder. If there are gaps in contact sheet numbering and the missing contacts are not located in the folders of removed contact sheets, then the contacts corresponding to the missing numbers were not acquired by the library and are not present in the collection.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in eight subseries: Early contact sheets, Portraits, Scapes, Series A, Series B, Series C, Series G, and Winifred.
Contact sheets within each subseries are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

did.unittitle: Removed early contact sheets and negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes early contact sheets removed from binders and corresponding 35mm black and white negatives. Removed contact sheets include 281, 390, 453, and 551. All of these items were found together in an envelope.

did.unittitle: Removed early contact sheets, negatives, and composite prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes early contact sheets removed from binders, corresponding 35mm black and white negatives, and a series of 8x10 prints depicting composite images created by Giard. Removed contact sheets include 131, 294, 371, 440, 447, 462, 477, and 534. All of these items were found together in an envelope.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives 134-185 include images from Mexico in 1973. See folders of removed contact sheets and negatives in this subseries for missing contact sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See folders of removed contact sheets and negatives in this subseries for missing contact sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See folders of removed contact sheets and negatives in this subseries for missing contact sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Date taken from corresponding negatives in Series II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes black and white images of landscapes and grounds including interior and exterior shots. Several of the original binders housing the contacts were labeled as "landscapes and grounds". Series G contact sheets are likely a continuation of the Scapes contacts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits and figures. For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes black and white portraits. For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes black and white portraits. For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes black and white images of landscapes and locations. Series G contact sheets are likely a continuation of the Scapes contacts, since the subject matter is similar and the numbering continues from where the Scapes series ends. "G" may indicate "Grounds".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes black and white contact sheets depicting portraits of the Thanks Be To Grandmother Winifred Foundation grant recipients, sometimes referred to as the Grandmother Winifred Portraits. Dates and locations for these contact sheets are taken from the corresponding negatives in Series II.

For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in New York, Massachusetts, Maine, New Hampshire, Vermont, and New Jersey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in New Jersey, California, Rhode Island, Oregon, Washington, Florida (including several images from the Crone's Cradle Conserve in Citra), Minnesota, Wisconsin, Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Michigan, Pennsylvania, the District of Columbia, Maryland, Virginia, Massachusetts, and New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in North Carolina, South Carolina, Florida, New Jersey, New York, Wisconsin, Illinois, Missouri, New Mexico, Arizona, Tennessee, Texas, California, Oklahoma, Arkansas, Colorado, Utah, Maine, Kentucky, Ohio, New Hampshire, Washington, and Alberta Canada.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in North Carolina, South Carolina, Florida, New Jersey, New York, Wisconsin, Illinois, Missouri, New Mexico, Arizona, Tennessee, Texas, California, Oklahoma, Arkansas, Colorado, Utah, Maine, Kentucky, Ohio, New Hampshire, Washington, and Alberta Canada.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in Massachusetts, New York, New Jersey, California, Florida, the District of Columbia, Maryland, and Virginia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in California, New Mexico, Colorado, New York, Vermont, and Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in Pennsylvania, Hawaii, Oregon, New York, Ohio, and Delaware.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Giard's negatives. For more information about subject names and image locations for negatives in the subseries Portraits, Series A, Series B, Series C, and Winifred, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). The spreadsheet is an export of Giard's Filemaker Pro database which cross-references negatives, contact sheets, and prints of his portraits and some landscapes and sites. See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

Included in Miscellaneous negatives, Portraits, and Series A are negatives that Giard removed from binders and placed in a safe deposit box, which are noted as "removed negatives". Removed negatives that are numbered with an identified photographer's series are foldered at the beginning of the photographer's series. Unnumbered removed negatives are in the Miscellaneous negatives subseries.

Negatives in folders that are labeled sequentially (i.e. 1 of 2, 2 of 2) were taken from the same binder. If there are gaps in negative numbering and the missing negatives are not located in the folders of removed negatives, then the negatives corresponding to the missing numbers were not acquired by the library and are not present in the collection.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in ten subseries: Miscellaneous negatives, Color negatives with contact sheets, Early negatives, Portraits, Scapes, Series A, Series B, Series C, Series G, and Winifred.
Miscellaneous negatives are arranged alphabetically by title and all other negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes miscellaneous negatives presumably taken by Giard. Removed negatives are those that Giard removed from the binders of negatives and placed in a safe deposit box. This subseries includes removed negatives that are unnumbered and for which the photographer's series is unknown. Titles are created by the archivist and are derived from the subject of images or notes written on original enclosures.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: 1- Apples, tennis, yard furniture and miscellaneous cut negative
Candid images of a group seated outdoors and portraits of a man
Decorative interiors, a garden, and the Taj Mahal
Exhibition with John Haigney - negatives and contact sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white negatives and contact sheets showing Giard's work that was featured in a collaborative exhibition with painter John Haigney at Southampton College in 1983-1984 as well as images of the exhibition itself.

did.unittitle: List of removed negatives placed in safe deposit box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a brief summary list of numbered negatives that Giard removed from the binders of negatives and placed in a safe deposit box.

did.unittitle: New York City - negatives and contact sheet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 35mm black and white negatives with notes about how each set of negatives was developed and a contact sheet labeled "Lisa Cresson, 2/28/1987, NYC #1, Tri X Nikkormat 24mm lens". Images depict portraits, figures, and a street event possibly in New York City.

did.unittitle: Portraits and Flatiron building, NYC - negatives and contact sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 120mm black and white negatives and contact sheets. These negatives depict portraits of a woman seated on a bench under a tree and a woman at a pottery wheel as well as the Flatiron building in New York City. The date is taken from a developer's invoice stored with the items. The images themselves may date from before 2002.

did.unittitle: Portraits of George Stambolian - negatives and contact sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white negatives and contact sheets.

did.unittitle: Portraits of man with a fan and nude

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 120mm color negatives. These negatives correspond with some slides in Series III: Slides, "Color slides, 1975-1988". The date for these negatives is derived from dates written on the slides.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These unnumbered 120mm black and white negatives were possibly removed from Series A, as they were stored with other negatives removed from Series A.

did.unittitle: Removed negatives - portrait of a man from behind

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These unnumbered 120mm black and white negatives were removed from either Series A or the Portraits series.

did.unittitle: Still lifes of Frisbees, toys, and a book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 inch by 5 inch acetate color negatives. These negatives correspond with some 35mm slides in Series III: Slides, "Color slides, 1975-1988". The date for these negatives is derived from dates written on the slides.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 120mm color negatives and associated contact sheets originally stored in three-ring binders.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images consist primarily of locations including India (March 1983, negative sheets 1-4), Amagansett, New York and Bridgehampton, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images of Giard's family, friends, travels, and locations where he lived including Hartford and New Haven, Connecticut and Amagansett, New York. Negatives 85-578 are sleeved in original booklets which include notes at the front of the booklet regarding people, locations, and dates of images. Negatives 43-84 were removed from original booklets, and the booklet notes are sleeved with the negatives. Giard noted his camera settings on some negative sleeves and booklets.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives depicting urban landscapes and buildings of Hartford and New Haven Connecticut and Yale University. Includes 120mm black and white negatives and 2.5 inch by 4.25 inch black and white negatives.

did.unittitle: 8 - Willow Street Hartford and NYC rubble
13 - NYC East River Promenade and area

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white negatives. Locations include Colombia, the Adirondacks, Vermont, West Virginia, Grand Teton National Park, and Amagansett, New York.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Negatives were rehoused in acid-free sleeves from original negative booklets. Giard's numbering system and descriptions were copied onto the new sleeves.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white negatives in seven original booklets. Locations include England, France, Connecticut, New York, Mexico, and Italy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white negatives in six original booklets. Location of images is primarily New York, including Amagansett. Includes candid images from farmer's markets, cemeteries, beaches, and highways, as well as holiday celebrations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white negatives in seven original booklets and one folder of sleeved negatives. Includes images of portraits, domestic scenes, and landscapes and grounds. Locations include Amagansett, New York, California, Montreal, the Adirondacks, and scenes from summer camps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

did.unittitle: 2-C Bill Atkin's place - 35mm color acetate negatives

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Found in box with Portrait negatives 1-284.

did.unittitle: Correspondence from Kay Tobin Lahusen to Giard about Barbara Gittings portraits - includes photograph and negatives

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Found in box with Portrait negatives 1-284.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 120mm black and white negatives in twenty-three original booklets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 120mm black and white negatives depicting suburban and urban landscapes and buildings. Most images are from New York locations including Amagansett, Southampton, East Hampton, and Montauk. Other locations include Syracuse, New York; the Adirondacks; New York City; Hartford, Connecticut; and Italy. Series G negatives are likely a continuation of Scapes negatives.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 120mm black and white negatives in sixteen original booklets. Several booklets include the letter "G" in the numbering system. This suggests that the Scapes series and Series G are related. "G" may indicate "Grounds".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 120mm black and white negatives primarily depicting portraits and figures. For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 120mm black and white negatives primarily depicting portraits and figures. For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 120mm black and white negatives primarily depicting portraits and figures. For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 120mm and 35mm black and white negatives. Primarily includes images of landscapes and locations. It is likely that Series G is a continuation of the Scapes series based on the numbering system and the fact that Scapes negatives 1-199 often have the letter "G" included in the numbering system written on the original booklets. "G" may indicate "Grounds".

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes still lifes, outdoor landscapes, interiors, and urban landscapes. A selection of locations include the Grand Ole Opry stage, a pride parade circa 1990, Giard's Particular Voices exhibit at the New York Public Library, Webster Hall in New York City, Stonewall, the Castro Theatre, the National Mall in Washington, DC, and several San Francisco locations including California Hall, the location of the former Black Cat Café, and the Twelve Adler Museum Café.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images of urban locations and gardens including Green River Cemetery in Springs, New York, a sculpture garden, and Paradise Garage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 120mm black and white negatives depicting portraits of the Thanks Be To Grandmother Winifred grant recipients. Giard wrote the names of subjects and the location of portraits on the negative sleeves. Locations are included in the description for each binder of negatives. For more information about subject names and image locations, see Giard's image spreadsheet linked here (Excel download). See also instructions for interpreting the spreadsheet linked here (Word download).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are arranged in numerical order according to Giard's numbering system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in New York, Massachusetts, Maine, New Hampshire, Vermont, and New Jersey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in New Jersey, California, Rhode Island, Oregon, Washington, Florida (including several images from the Crone's Cradle Conserve in Citra), Minnesota, Wisconsin, Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Michigan, Pennsylvania, the District of Columbia, Maryland, Virginia, Massachusetts, and New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in North Carolina, South Carolina, Florida, New Jersey, New York, Wisconsin, Illinois, Missouri, New Mexico, Arizona, Tennessee, Texas, California, Oklahoma, Arkansas, Colorado, Utah, Maine, Kentucky, Ohio, New Hampshire, Washington, and Alberta Canada.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in Massachusetts, New York, New Jersey, California, Florida, the District of Columbia, Maryland, and Virginia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in California, New Mexico, Colorado, New York, Vermont, and Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits taken in Pennsylvania, Hawaii, Oregon, New York, Ohio, and Delaware.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Giard's slides depicting his portraits, landscapes, figures, travels, and other work and includes 35mm and 2.25 inch slides in both color and black and white. Descriptive titles are derived from titles written on binders and boxes that originally housed the slides.
Note that many dates are taken from dates printed on the slides, though some dates were written on slides by Giard. The images depicted in slides may be older than the date the slide was created.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm color slides depicting non-photographic art installations in a school classroom.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm color slides depicting non-photographic art installations in a school classroom.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm color slides depicting a non-photographic art installation with what appear to be paper dolls and collage art.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides of landscapes and grounds in Amagansett, NY (1975-1977) and portraits unidentified people and objects (1974-1976). Slides originally in a binder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm and 2.25 inch color slides from a binder. Includes portraits, still lifes, landscapes, and interiors. Includes images of "Chairs of the Twentieth Century" which appeared in the January-February 1977 issue of Architectural Digest. Title is taken from writing on the binder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2.25 inch and 35mm color slides from a binder. Several 2.25 inch slides correspond with negatives in Series II: Negatives, Miscellaneous negatives, "Portraits of man with a fan and nude, 1979". Some 35mm negatives correspond with negatives in Series II: Negatives, Miscellaneous negatives, "Still lifes of Frisbees, toys, and a book, circa 1978".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 75 35mm black and white slides labeled with titles and dates. The portraits are sequentially numbered on the sleeve.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm and 2.25 inch color slides depicting building interiors.

did.unittitle: Man camping and hiking in the mountains
Miscellaneous portraits, figures, and still lifes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides that were originally loose in a box labeled "Slides, Activists & Such".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides from a binder. Includes a statement by Giard about his portrait series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white 35mm slides from a binder. Images of suburban and rural landscapes, gardens, and woods.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 35mm and 2.25 inch color slides showing Giard's work that was featured in a collaborative exhibition with painter John Haigney at Southampton College in 1983-1984 as well as images of the exhibition itself. Portraits date from 1977. Slides originally stored in a binder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides from a binder. First slide sleeve is labeled: "Figures in landscape (includes portraits of Bill King, Connie Fox, Val Telberg, Lelia Ketayen, Siv Cedering, Joe Pintauro, Sandra Weiner, Amy Zerner)"

did.unittitle: Portraits and nudes from the Portrait series - Record of sales

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides from a binder. Includes self-portraits of Giard, "beach parts", portraits of others, and nudes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2.25 inch color slides depicting portraits, landscapes, and still lifes.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Slides were originally in six small Kodak boxes that were associated together. Slides have been placed in sleeves, and the sleeves are labeled to indicate which slides were boxed together. Box numbers have been arbitrarily assigned and do not indicate a consecutive order of the slides.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits, interiors, locations, and still lifes. Locations include Hartford, Connecticut and New York. Portraits include Michael Graves and a girl in a pink tutu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 33mm and 2.25 inch color slides from a binder. Includes a table of contents listing locations in India. These slides do not appear to correspond directly with the color negative sheets 1-4 in Series II: Negatives, Subseries II: Color negatives. However, the images may be from the same trip to India, since both the slides and color negatives are dated 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides from a binder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm color slides depicting decorative interiors. The two original boxes of slides were labeled as "SH/ARCH".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm color slides depicting decorative interiors. The two original boxes of slides were labeled as "Vahradian".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides from a binder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides depicting portraits of novelist Gertrude Stein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides depicting what appears to be the interior of the Ellis Island hospital and exterior of the National Immigration Museum. Original box of slides was labeled "Ellis". Slides correspond with negatives in Series II: Negatives, Scapes 660-664.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides of Giard's photographic series depicting landscapes and art installations at The Creeks, the estate of artist Alfonso Ossorio. Slides are dated 1991 and 2001. Includes correspondence with the Queens Museum of Art (dated 2005) about slides used for catalog illustrations and correspondence with the Ossorio Foundation (dated 2000) about the use of three photographs of The Creeks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white negatives originally stored in three small slide boxes labeled "Albany". These slides depict images of an exhibition of Giard's work, possibly at the University Art Gallery at SUNY Albany in 1993.

did.unittitle: Oscar Wilde gravesite and miscellaneous portraits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides depicting Oscar Wilde's gravesite as well as a series of portraits. The original box for these slides was labeled "Wilde" and "Still Gay 9/93".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides depicting portraits of activist José Sarria and images of Barney's Beanery in California.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides depicting portraits of activist Mabel Hampton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm black and white slides depicting miscellaneous portraits. The names listed on the original box include Nichols, Shelley, Kameny, Leitsch, Dorr, Call, Bi, Cameron, Sarria, Hayib (?), Storme, One arm, Lucas, Vincenz, and Ben. The box was also labeled "Extras".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 35mm black and white slides and correspondence from Giard to Liz Sullivan, Senior Editor at Rizzoli International Publications, Inc. regarding a proposal to create a book of Giard's portraits. Included is a list of portraits, slides of the portraits, and a copy of Giard's CV. Slides and correspondence originally stored in a binder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes Giard's project notes, correspondence, and financial papers; printed material relating to exhibitions of his work; magazines featuring his work; and annotated road maps and guides from his travels.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically.

did.unittitle: Biographical information about Giard and his work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings, newspaper clippings, and exhibition printed material relating to Giard and his work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folders arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of a collaborative exhibition featuring Giard's work and the work of painter John Haigney at Southampton College in 1983-1984.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an exhibit catalog for On the Domestic Front: Scenes of Everyday Queer Life - a 2015 exhibit at the Leslie-Lohman Museum of Gay and Lesbian Art- and a guide for the 2012 Scotiabank CONTACT Photography Festival featuring Giard's work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Giard's business income and tax paperwork.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folders arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Gay and Lesbian slide show notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material relating to a slide show of portraits from Giard's Particular Voices project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographic prints and printed matter featuring Giard's images of building interiors and furniture. Includes images features in Architectural Digest issues 1975-1977.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folders arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes project notes, lists of Giard's prints, artist statements, newspaper clippings, and notes on a slide presentation.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folders arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes descriptions of the New York Public Library's collection of Giard's photographs, including acquisition paperwork, and a NYPL Gay and Lesbian Studies research guide.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folders arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Paperweight from the James C. Hormel Gay and Lesbian Center in the San Francisco Public Library
Particular Voices: Portraits of Gay and Lesbian Writers project material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes biographies, address lists and lists of names of portrait subjects; printed materials relating to Particular Voices ; literature catalogs; and a 1996 publication agreement with MIT Press.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folders arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes journals, magazines, and newspapers featuring articles about Giard, Giard's photographs, and advertising Giard's exhibits. Publications include The Advocate, Architectural Digest, The Bridgehampton Sun, Camera, Art Now/New York Gallery Guide, Creative Camera, Christopher Street, Contact Sheet: The Light Work Annual, The East Hampton Star, Gai Pied, The Gay and Lesbian Review, The James White Review, The New Yorker, Photo World , and TimeOut New York .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Publications arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes journals, magazines, and newspapers featuring articles about Giard, Giard's photographs, and advertising Giard's exhibits. Publications include The Advocate, Architectural Digest, The Bridgehampton Sun, Camera, Art Now/New York Gallery Guide, Creative Camera, Christopher Street, Contact Sheet: The Light Work Annual, The East Hampton Star, Gai Pied, The Gay and Lesbian Review, The James White Review, The New Yorker, Photo World , and TimeOut New York .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Publications arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: Robert Giard Foundation event printed material
San Francisco Public Library event materials and photograph acquisition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes event materials and correspondence and lists pertaining to the San Francisco Public Library's acquisition of a number of Giard's photographic prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes annotated road maps, public transportation maps, travel guides, and news clippings from Giard's business and personal travel throughout the United States, Canada, and Europe. Of note are LGBT travel material including The Book of Maps and Columbia Fun Maps .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folders arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes annotated travel materials, newsclippings, and Giard's notes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maps are arranged as received.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maps are arranged as received.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maps are arranged as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition contains printed matter and press coverage from events hosted by the Robert Giard Foundation including exhibit and event flyers, printed news articles about a 2019 exhibit of Giard's work at Daniel Cooney Fine Art in New York City, and program books for Robert Giard Foundation Fellowship events.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This acquisition is arranged as received.

did.unittitle: Robert Giard Foundation event printed material and press coverage
